hero.heading: Welcome to Luke's International Fellowship & Enrichment - LIFE

sections.body: <font size="3" color="#000000">We are a faith-based, non-profit network of Christian healthcare professionals dedicated to serving God and fulfilling the Great Commission. Rooted in Christian values, LIFE engages in medical missions, health education, community outreach, holistic well being and spiritual discipleship — bringing healing, hope, and transformation to individuals and communities worldwide.</font>
sections.title: About Luke's International Fellowship & Enrichment - LIFE

sections.body: To share the gospel of Jesus Christ through healthcare. It aims to strengthen healthcare professionals in their walk with God, equip them with the skills of an Evangelist, and provide opportunities to be an effective witness of Jesus Christ.
sections.title: Our Mission

items.name: Medical Missions
items.description: Local and overseas, bringing clinical services to the poor and undeserved in partnership with communities, hospitals and medical schools.

items.name: Spiritual Fellowship
items.description: Provide services that encourage, strengthen and foster a community of Christian healthcare professionals.

items.name: Medical Education
items.description: Mentoring through individual and group sessions, seminars for continuing medical education, scholarships and medical training opportunities both locally and internationally.

sections.title: Some glimpses of our medical mission

sections.body: When you support us, you’re not just giving – you’re healing, you’re lifting, you’re standing in the gap for those who are unseen and unheard. Your help becomes a hand to the sick, a voice to the voiceless, and hope to the hopeless. Be the miracle someone is praying for today.
sections.title: Support us

sections.body: Duncan Hospital experience
sections.title: Blog Insights

items.answer: LIFE is a Christ-centered mission with a primary focus on medical outreach and compassionate care. We organize medical camps, health support initiatives, and humanitarian aid for underserved communities, while also providing spiritual encouragement and discipleship. Alongside healthcare outreach, we conduct Bible teaching, mentorship programs, and mission partnerships that bring both physical healing and spiritual hope. Our mission is to serve people holistically — body, mind, and spirit.
items.question: What services does Luke's International Fellowship & Enrichment - LIFE offer?

items.answer: You can get involved with LIFE by partnering with our medical outreach and mission programs. Volunteers, healthcare professionals, supporters, and prayer partners are all welcome. Simply reach out through our contact page to join an outreach team, sponsor a project, or participate in our fellowship and training programs. We’ll guide you in finding the best way to serve and make an impact.
items.question: How can I get started?

items.answer: What makes LIFE unique is our integration of medical care and Christ-centered mission. We combine practical healthcare support with spiritual mentorship and discipleship. Our outreach is driven by compassion, faith, and long-term community impact — not just short-term aid. We aim to build relationships, restore dignity, and share hope through both healing service and the message of Christ.
items.question: What makes you different?

sections.body: Ready for a hand with Luke's International Fellowship & Enrichment - LIFE? We'd love to hear from you.
sections.title: Contact

sections.body: Luke’s International Fellowship and Enrichment, Inc is a nonprofit corporation of Christian health care professionals that is organized exclusively for religious, educational, and charitable purposes as defined under 501(c)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code. The purpose of this organization is to bring healing to the body and Spirit by delivering medical care and sharing the good news of the gospel of Jesus Christ. We seek to accomplish these ends by mobilizing resources and medical expertise both in the United States and around the world with a special focus on the South Asian subcontinent.The objectives of this corporation are to foster the spiritual growth of Christian health care professionals, promote the development of health care professionals to be effective witnesses of Jesus Christ, conduct and support short and long term medical missions both locally and internationally, provide continuing medical education for healthcare medical professionals germane to the objectives of this corporation,promote public health efforts directly and by dissemination of medical literature both independently and in collaboration with other like-minded institutions, provide networking opportunities for like-minded individuals to greater fulfill the objectives of LIFE.The individual and summative effect of these objectives will be to improve the health and health awareness of those we serve and to fulfill the Great Commission as directed by Jesus Christ.
sections.title: About Us

sections.body: LIFE aims to strengthen Christian health care professionals in their walk with God, equip them with the skills to be an evangelist for Christ in healthcare, and provide opportunities for them to be an effective witness of Jesus Christ.
sections.title: The Mission of LIFE is to share the gospel of Jesus Christ through Healthcare

sections.body:
We Believe:
  • In one God eternally existing in three persons: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit.
  • That Jesus Christ is the only begotten Son of the Father, conceived of the Holy Ghost, and born of the Virgin Mary.
  • That Jesus was crucified, buried, and raised from the dead; that He ascended to heaven and is today at the right hand of the Father as the Intercessor.
  • In the personality of the Holy Spirit, and that as promised, He came down from Heaven on the day of Pentecost, to dwell permanently in each believer, and that He is the efficient power for godly living, Christian service, and spiritual worship.
  • In the Divine inspiration and authority of the Holy Scriptures.
  • That all have sinned and come short of the glory of God.
  • That justification and sanctification of the believer through the finished work of Christ.
  • In water baptism by immersion, and all who repent should be baptized in the name of the Father, and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit.
  • In the Lord’s Supper.
  • In the baptism of the Holy Spirit for believers.
  • In Divine healing provided for all in the atonement.
  • That God wonderfully and immutably creates each person as male or female. These two distinct, complementary genders together reflect the image and nature of God. Rejection of one’s biological sex is a rejection of the image of God within that person.
  • In a marriage between one man and one woman in a single, exclusive union. We believe that God intends sexual intimacy to occur only between a man and a woman who are married to each other. 
  • We believe that God has commanded that no intimate sexual activity be engaged in outside of marriage between a man and a woman.
  • That all human life is sacred and created in His image. That human life is of priceless worth in all its dimensions, including pre-born babies, the aged, the physically or mentally challenged, and every other stage or condition from conception through natural death, That we are therefore called to defend, protect and value all human life.
  • That any form of sexual immorality (including adultery, fornication, homosexual behavior, bisexual conduct, bestiality, incest, and use of pornography) is sinful and offensive to God.
  • In the second coming of Jesus Christ.
  • In the bodily resurrection; eternal life for the righteous and eternal punishment for the wicked.
  • In the millennial reign of Jesus Christ and in the new heavens and new earth.

sections.body:
As you may have heard, India is amid a crisis of the second wave of Covid which is 3.5 times higher than the peak of the first wave. The whole medical infrastructure has collapsed and India is in dire need of resources like oxygen concentrators that provide portable oxygen to all those affected by Covid 19. Many are dying due to a severe shortage in oxygen at hospitals and will benefit from 5 to 10 liters per minute (LPM) of oxygen supply.
 

We, at Luke’s International Fellowship and Enrichment (LIFE) have decided to partner with an organization called Friends of Agape based in Colorado Springs, CO that will help provide for these immediate oxygen needs through your generous giving.


Sometimes, we are not sure of how to make an impact around us with the resources we have. Supporting this cause is a great way to make sure that our light is “like a city on a hilltop that cannot be hidden. No one lights a lamp and then puts it under a basket. Instead, a lamp is placed on a stand, where it gives light to everyone in the house. In the same way, let your good deeds shine for all to see, so that everyone will praise your Heavenly Father” Matt 5:14-16. A little help will go a long way as we request that you prayerfully give to this cause to help alleviate the pain and desperation of our fellow brothers and sisters in India at this time. We hope that your small gesture of giving will be the light that turns India to Jesus!
Join us to help India by donating today!

sections.title: Impacted Kenya!
Video Story of Kenya Mission Trip!

sections.body:
Over the course of four days, we successfully treated over 600 patients. Additionally, we treated 30 abandoned children performed x-ray guided pain procedures, assisted with spine surgery, observed  open-heart surgery, lectured to university medical students and faculty and taught medical students, but above all, we brought the gospel of Christ to the unreached.

His name be glorified! Christ is All!

sections.title: About Us

sections.body:
Luke's International Fellowship & Enrichment – LIFE
Website: www.lifeint.org

Last Updated: March 2026

Luke's International Fellowship & Enrichment – LIFE (“LIFE”, “we”, “our”, or “us”) values your trust and is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, protect, and share your personal information when you visit www.lifeint.org or interact with our ministry online.

1. Information We Collect

We may collect information when you:

  • Visit our website

  • Submit forms

  • Subscribe to updates

  • Contact us

  • Register for events

  • Make donations

  • Volunteer or partner with our ministry

Personal Information

This may include:

  • Full name

  • Email address

  • Phone number

  • Mailing address

  • Organization or church affiliation

  • Donation information

  • Payment transaction details

Donation Information

When you make a donation through our website, we may collect:

  • Donor name

  • Email and contact information

  • Donation amount

  • Billing address

  • Payment information (processed through secure third-party payment processors)

Important: LIFE does not store full credit card numbers on our servers.

Technical Information

We may also automatically collect:

  • IP address

  • Browser type

  • Device information

  • Pages visited

  • Time spent on pages

  • Referring website

This information helps us improve our website and ministry outreach.

2. How We Use Your Information

We use the information we collect to:

  • Process and acknowledge donations

  • Send donation receipts

  • Communicate ministry updates

  • Share newsletters and prayer updates

  • Respond to inquiries

  • Register users for events or programs

  • Improve website functionality

  • Maintain records required for nonprofit compliance

  • Provide supporters with ministry reports and updates

3. Donations

Donations made through www.lifeint.org help support the mission and outreach programs of Luke's International Fellowship & Enrichment – LIFE.

Your donations may support activities such as:

  • Christian ministry outreach

  • Leadership training

  • Fellowship and spiritual enrichment programs

  • Mission support initiatives

  • Community service and humanitarian work

Donors may receive:

  • A confirmation email

  • An official donation receipt for tax purposes (if applicable)

All donations are voluntary and non-refundable, unless an error occurs during the donation transaction.

4. Payment Processing

Online donations may be processed through trusted third-party payment providers such as:

  • Stripe

  • PayPal

  • Donor management platforms

These providers handle payment information securely and in compliance with industry standards.

Please review their privacy policies separately if you use their services.

5. Sharing Your Information

We do not sell, rent, or trade donor information.

We may share limited information with trusted service providers for:

  • Payment processing

  • Email communication systems

  • Website hosting services

  • Donation management tools

  • Legal or regulatory compliance

All partners must maintain confidentiality and data security.

6. Cookies and Tracking

Our website may use cookies and analytics tools to:

  • Understand visitor behavior

  • Improve website performance

  • Enhance user experience

Users can disable cookies in their browser settings.

7. Data Security

We take reasonable measures to protect personal data, including:

  • Secure website hosting

  • SSL encryption

  • Limited data access

  • Secure payment processing

However, no online system is completely secure, and users should exercise caution when submitting information online.

8. Email Communication

If you provide your email address, we may send:

  • Ministry updates

  • Prayer newsletters

  • Event announcements

  • Donation confirmations

You may unsubscribe from emails at any time.

9. Children's Privacy

Our website is not directed toward children under 13.
We do not knowingly collect personal information from children.

10. External Links

Our website may contain links to other websites or ministries.
We are not responsible for the privacy practices of those sites.

11. Changes to This Policy

LIFE may update this Privacy Policy periodically.
Updates will be posted on this page with the revised date.

12. Contact Information

If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or your information, please contact:

Luke's International Fellowship & Enrichment – LIFE
Website: www.lifeint.org

Email: orglife2020@gmail.com

7. Limitation of Liability

8. Governing Law

These Terms are governed by the laws of the United States and the applicable state jurisdiction.

9. Changes to Terms

We may update these Terms at any time.
Changes will be posted on this page.

10. Contact

For questions regarding these Terms, please contact:

Luke's International Fellowship & Enrichment – LIFE

sections.title: Terms

sections.body: The Leprosy Mission Trust India (TLMTI), founded in 1874 as the ‘Mission to Lepers’ by anIrishman named Wellesley Cosby Bailey. The organization works with people affected by leprosy and other neglected tropical diseases (NTDs), people with disabilities and marginalized communities. The Leprosy Mission (TLM) Hospital Faizabad was established in the year of 1938, in the District of Ayodhya (formerly Faizabad) in the state of Uttar Pradesh, India. They see 300 outpatients daily, have 30 in-patient beds, 15 of which are assigned to people affected by leprosy which is regularly over capacity. Reconstructive surgery (RCS) for persons affected by leprosy is one of the unique services for which patients from 25 Districts are referred to TLM Hospital Faizabad performing 80 - 100 RCS/year along with pre and post-surgical rehabilitation. There are also General Surgery camps in every quarter provided at an affordable cost in addition to minor surgical procedures on daily basis (daily 10 – 15). TLM has managed with an old autoclave (high pressures sterilizer for surgical instruments) over 20 years old which has had several performance concerns requiring multiple repair and part replacements and most recently has raised concern for risk of injury to those who operate the machine. Due to financial constraints, TLM Hospital Faizabad has not been able to purchase a new autoclave. Additionally, there was no commercial laundry washer or dryer in the hospital.
sections.title: The Leprosy Mission Hospital, Faizabad

sections.body: LIFE was able to successfully raise $12,000, directed to The Leprosy Mission for the purchase of the autoclave and commercial laundry system (washer and dryer.)
sections.title: About Us

sections.body:
I walked through the entrance of a dark sandstone fortress, the entrance to Duncan Hospital in the state of Bihar, India. Built by Christian Scottish Missionaries in the 1920s, the contrast between its walls were startling. The missionaries had chosen a dilapidated place like Bihar for their mission. Stepping inside resembled the comforting warm air of walking into a home from the freezing January air. Outside it was disorganized, dirty and chaotic yet inside the hospital campus was organized, clean and orderly. I was greeted by friends and informed I had just missed the Joni and Friends mission team by two days.

My friends took me on a campus tour where I saw buildings with a contemporary façade. One magnificent building was donated by a famous British Orthopedic Surgeon touched through his experiences at Duncan decades earlier. The campus pathways were surrounded by gardens with flowers and exotic trees. These were donated by an American ER Physician who spent several years volunteering in Bihar. The gardeners tended these flowers and gardens, a continuation of his legacy of love to the community. I was surprised to learn this remote rural hospital saw inflows of volunteers from around the world including Europe, UK and the United States. The volunteers encompassed healthcare professionals not limited to: multi-specialty physicians, critical care nurses, physical therapists, residents and students of all healthcare disciplines.


Walking through organized flower and tree garden pathways, I was taken to the private residences comprising three-story buildings stacked in blocks facing each other. Stairways provided access to each floor and I could see the children playing in between the staircases. I retired for the night. The five-hour journey from the capital exhausted me yet I was excited in anticipation for the next few days at Duncan.


Next morning I woke up to the rooster crow, an unconventional alarm, prompting me to rise up for the day. I had a delicious morning tea made from whole buffalo milk packed with nutrition. This was a daily routine for the Bihari population whose meals were otherwise nutrient poor. After a hot breakfast I went with my host for the morning prayer and sign-out from the overnight medical team. Sign-out was a tradition I was familiar with from my residency days. The night-shift doctors discussed cases requiring critical attention with the day team to prioritize the needs for the day.


After sign-out, I was assigned to work with the Ob/Gyn service. One of the physician’s I would meet was recruited to meet the shortage in the busiest unit in the hospital i.e. Obstetrics. The Bihari population was exploding and the state did not have enough physicians to meet woman’s health needs. Families had multiple children driven by illiteracy and misperceptions. The Obstetric unit was a long and wide hallway partitioned by curtains, each having a bed with a mother in a different stage of labor. There were about 40 mothers about to deliver their baby. While one mother was being induced, another was in active labor and yet in another bed a baby was being born with the assistance of a midwife. This pattern continued from one bed to the next.


At the end of the hallway I met the lead Obstetrician. She was surrounded by several trainees impulsively waiting to inform her about the looming C sections and emergencies. While I politely introduced myself a resident physician interrupted me informing her that a “STAT” or emergency C-section was en route to the OR. A baby was having concerning decreases in the heart rate called decelerations and its laboring mother was getting fatigued. This can be a critical period for the wellbeing of both the baby and mother.

The lead physician directed her attention to me and asked, “Doc would you assist me?” I had delivered babies during my medical school rotation. The old adage sprung to my mind “Often Wrong, Never in Doubt”. But was there an alternative? There was a dire need for medical help in managing the overwhelming volume of patients. Who else would assist if not me at that moment? I exclaimed “Yes, of course, let’s go, I’m ready”.


I was escorted to the scrub room, where sterile cloth scrubs were kept. I gowned up and went to the OR. I assisted by holding the suction cannula in one hand and a surgical scissor in the other. I got involved to the degree I felt comfortable. One of the resident physicians was a recent graduate. He was not fellowship trained, yet he grew through the circumstances enabling him to take on a fellow’s role delivering 100s of babies over a short time span. I paused to ponder the dire lack of physician resources and how necessity makes an individual grow into a role. I recalled those moments delivering babies during my Ob/Gyn medical school rotation and was excited to relive them again. More importantly the camaraderie of the attendings and the residents made me feel welcome as a part of the family. The baby was successfully delivered and mother and baby did well.


After a busy day, I took a stroll through the campus. Nudged in a corner was the Shanthi Niwas (peaceful home), a makeshift hostel for international guests volunteering at the hospital. Well-equipped rooms with Wi-fi and even a common furnished dining area was prearranged to keep the guests comfortable during their stay. Guests came to the dining area for a good meal, fun conversations and to discuss their clinical experiences that day. The Joni and Friends team had just left the campus leaving the Shanti Niwas near empty. There was a lone visiting nurse from Denmark volunteering at the hospital. She was reminiscing the fun times with the Joni and Friends team and their energy. Like many other visiting guests, she chose to volunteer in an impoverished area. She inquired with her hospital in Denmark for an international volunteer opportunity and they recommended Duncan hospital. The hospital receiving excellent feedback from the Danish visiting faculty established itself as a strong exchange site for their students.


The following day was community service day, where physicians volunteered to meet the needs of the surrounding communities. I met a physician in his mid-forties who was away from his family volunteering for about 6 months. Periodically hospitals could request short term commitment from doctors to meet temporary physician shortages. Duncan requested volunteer physician services through a healthcare recruitment firm. He decided to volunteer and his family supported his short-term commitment. He missed his family, however expressed joy in working with the people of Bihar and participating with the missionary community at Duncan.


One day I was told I had a surprise. I was taken to the kitchen where steamed dumplings called Momo were prepared for the OR team and guests. It was a celebration after an intense work day where staff and senior physicians got together to enjoy this delicacy. We prayed and dug in eating several of these rounded ball delicacies.


My few days quickly went by: morning rounds, ICU service, delivering a baby, community services, eating momo and forging new friendships. I would miss these moments. I got ready to board the sleeper class train to Aurangabad. I boarded the train and said goodbye to my hosts as the train left the station. I settled to relive the memories which had left a vivid impression in my head.


Bihar was the lowest in the Index of health advancement on multiple metrics and the poorest of states to settle as a family. Yet a century ago Scottish Missionaries chose the neediest place to give their best. They were zealous for the poor just like Paul on his missions to spread the gospel. I had left this hospital transformed in my soul more than my mind. It was a spiritual healing and a timely stop on my journey.




-Reflections of Dr. Tony George at The Duncan Hospital, Raxaul Bihar

Clinical Clerkship Strengths:

Obstetrics

Gynecology

Neonatology

Intensive Care

Community Medicine

Website: https://www.duncanhospital-eha.org/


Duncan Contact : Dr Prabhu Joseph

Email address : prabhujoseph@gmail.com

items.answer: LIFE is a Christ-centered mission with a primary focus on medical outreach and compassionate care. We organize medical camps, health support initiatives, and humanitarian aid for underserved communities, while also providing spiritual encouragement and discipleship. Alongside healthcare outreach, we conduct Bible teaching, mentorship programs, and mission partnerships that bring both physical healing and spiritual hope. Our mission is to serve people holistically — body, mind, and spirit.
items.question: What services does Luke's International Fellowship & Enrichment - LIFE offer?

items.answer: You can get involved with LIFE by partnering with our medical outreach and mission programs. Volunteers, healthcare professionals, supporters, and prayer partners are all welcome. Simply reach out through our contact page to join an outreach team, sponsor a project, or participate in our fellowship and training programs. We’ll guide you in finding the best way to serve and make an impact.
items.question: How can I get started?

items.answer: What makes LIFE unique is our integration of medical care and Christ-centered mission. We combine practical healthcare support with spiritual mentorship and discipleship. Our outreach is driven by compassion, faith, and long-term community impact — not just short-term aid. We aim to build relationships, restore dignity, and share hope through both healing service and the message of Christ.
items.question: What makes you different?

sections.body: Ready for a hand with Luke's International Fellowship & Enrichment - LIFE? We'd love to hear from you.
